title: Heterocoma erecta
taxonomy: Heterocoma erecta is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the tribe Heliantheae. The genus Heterocoma is a part of the subfamily Asteroideae. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and phylogenetic relationships with other plants in the same family. Further research may provide more specific information on its classification within the Asteraceae family.

intro: Heterocoma erecta, also known as "Mexican Heterocoma," is a perennial herb native to Mexico and Central America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Heterocoma erecta include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
